 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vqr","title":"Create SSH troubleshooting guide","description":"# Task: Create SSH Troubleshooting Guide

## Description
Comprehensive guide for common SSH connection failures.

## Location
- Website: Embedded in wizard step 6
- Installer: ~/.acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md

## Common Issues Covered

### Permission denied (publickey)
**Symptoms:** `Permission denied (publickey)`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong key file specified
2. Key not added to VPS
3. Wrong username

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Verify key exists
ls -la ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519*

# Try with verbose output
ssh -v -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP
```

### Connection refused
**Symptoms:** `Connection refused`
**Causes:**
1. SSH service not running
2. Firewall blocking port 22
3. Wrong IP address

**Fixes:**
- Check IP is correct
- VPS provider may need firewall rule

### Connection timeout
**Symptoms:** `Connection timed out`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong IP
2. VPS not fully booted
3. Network issue

**Fixes:**
- Wait 2-3 minutes for VPS to boot
- Verify IP in provider dashboard

### Host key verification failed
**Symptoms:** `Host key verification failed`
**Causes:** 
- VPS was recreated with same IP
- Man-in-the-middle warning

**Fixes:**
```bash
ssh-keygen -R IP_ADDRESS
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All common issues documented
- [ ] Clear cause → fix structure
- [ ] Commands are copy-paste ready
- [ ] Integrated in website step 6","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","labels":["documentation","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","title":"EPIC: Website Wizard MVP","description":"# Epic: Website Wizard MVP

## Overview
Build the step-by-step wizard website that guides beginners from "I have a laptop" to "paste the curl|bash command".

## Background & Reasoning
The wizard is the user's first touchpoint with ACFS. It must:
- Feel premium and trustworthy
- Never overwhelm with jargon
- Make every step feel achievable
- Handle both Mac and Windows users
- Guide through VPS provider selection

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router
- Bun runtime
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- Vercel hosting + Cloudflare caching

## Key Design Principles
1. **Single action per screen** - One button or one command
2. **Copy button on every command** - Reduce friction
3. **"I did it" checkbox** - Gates "Next" button
4. **Collapsible troubleshooting** - Don't clutter but be ready

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 10 wizard steps implemented
- [ ] OS-aware command rendering
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] Lighthouse score > 90
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)

## Relationship to Project Goals
The wizard is the "front door" to ACFS. A confused or frustrated user at this stage will never reach the VPS installer. Excellence here directly enables the 10-minute promise.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","labels":["epic","website"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.1","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 App Router project","description":"# Task: Initialize Next.js 16 App Router Project

## Description
Set up the Next.js 16 project in apps/web/ with:
- App Router structure
- TypeScript configuration
- Tailwind CSS
- shadcn/ui components

## Technical Details
```bash
cd apps/web
bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir
bunx shadcn-ui@latest init
```

## Configuration
- Use Bun as package manager
- Configure for Vercel deployment
- Set up path aliases (@/components, @/lib)
- Configure next.config.js for optimal settings

## Reasoning
Next.js 16 with App Router provides:
- Modern React Server Components
- Automatic code splitting
- Optimal performance defaults
- Easy Vercel deployment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Next.js 16 project created
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled
- [ ] Tailwind CSS working
- [ ] shadcn/ui initialized
- [ ] bun run dev works
- [ ] bun run build succeeds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:18.88745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, and turbopack.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.11","title":"Implement wizard Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 6 - SSH Connection

## Description
Help users make their first SSH connection to the VPS.

## Why This Is Critical
This is where most beginners fail:
- Wrong username (root vs ubuntu)
- Key permissions wrong
- Firewall blocking port 22
- Host key verification confusion

## Key Elements
- Command using stored IP from step 5
- OS-aware command (Mac vs Windows)
- "Try ubuntu@, if fails try root@" guidance
- Host key verification explanation
- Comprehensive troubleshooting section

## Troubleshooting Covered
- Permission denied (publickey)
- Connection refused
- Connection timeout
- Host key verification failed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command uses stored IP
- [ ] OS-specific variants shown
- [ ] ubuntu/root fallback explained
- [ ] Troubleshooting expandable section","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.524339-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.524339-05:00","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.11","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.526273-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.12","title":"Implement wizard Step 7: Paste the ACFS One-Liner","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 7 - The Magic Moment

## Description
The climactic step where users paste the installer command.

## Why This Matters
This is the entire point of ACFS. Make it feel:
- Special (this is the magic)
- Safe (explain what it does)
- Transparent (link to source)

## Key Elements
- Large, prominent command card
- Giant copy button
- "What this will do" section (collapsible)
- Link to install.sh source on GitHub
- Expected duration (~10-15 minutes)
- "Don't close this terminal" warning

## The Command
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command prominently displayed
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] "What this does" expandable
- [ ] Link to source code
- [ ] Duration estimate shown","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.828318-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.828318-05:00","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.12","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.828743-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.13","title":"Implement wizard Step 8: Reconnect as Ubuntu","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 8 - Reconnect as Ubuntu

## Description
If user ran installer as root, guide them to reconnect as ubuntu.

## Key Elements
- Conditional display (only if started as root)
- Simple exit + reconnect command
- Verification that shell is now zsh
- Skip button if already ubuntu

## Commands
```bash
exit
ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

## Why This Step Exists
The installer creates/normalizes the ubuntu user. If they started as root,
they need to reconnect to get the full zsh experience.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Conditional display logic
- [ ] Skip option for ubuntu users
- [ ] Reconnect command with stored IP
- [ ] Verify new shell is zsh","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:32.653128-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:32.653128-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.13","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:32.655059-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
